textbook_scenarios.name: OGTT
textbook_scenarios.description: 75g oral glucose tolerance test — glucose peak/recovery, insulin response, glucagon suppression

checks.name: glucose_rises
checks.description: Glucose should rise >20 mg/dL above baseline after 75g load

checks.name: glucose_peak_timing
checks.description: Glucose peak should occur 10-90 min after load (t=40-120)

checks.name: glucose_returns
checks.description: Glucose should return below 140 mg/dL by 150 min post-load (t=180)

checks.name: insulin_rises
checks.description: Insulin should rise meaningfully in response to glucose

checks.name: glucagon_suppressed
checks.description: Glucagon should be lower during glucose absorption than at baseline

textbook_scenarios.name: overnight_fast
textbook_scenarios.description: 14-hour overnight fast — glucose maintenance, ketogenesis onset, hunger signaling

checks.name: glucose_stays_viable
checks.description: Glucose should remain above 60 mg/dL even after 14h fast

checks.name: glucose_declines
checks.description: Late-fast glucose should be lower than early post-meal

checks.name: insulin_low
checks.description: Insulin should be near basal (<15 μU/mL) by end of fast

checks.name: ffa_rises
checks.description: Free fatty acids should be higher at end of fast than postprandially

checks.name: bhb_rises
checks.description: β-Hydroxybutyrate should rise as fat oxidation increases

checks.name: ghrelin_rises
checks.description: Ghrelin (hunger) should increase during prolonged fast

textbook_scenarios.name: meal_dose_response
textbook_scenarios.description: Carbohydrate dose-response: 30g vs 90g meal — glucose, insulin, GLP-1 scaling

checks.name: glucose_dose_response
checks.description: 90g carb meal should produce higher glucose peak than 30g

checks.name: insulin_dose_response
checks.description: 90g carb meal should produce higher insulin peak than 30g

checks.name: glp1_dose_response
checks.description: Larger meal should produce higher GLP-1 response

textbook_scenarios.name: exercise_bout
textbook_scenarios.description: 30-minute moderate exercise bout — cardiovascular, metabolic, and thermal responses

checks.name: hr_rises_during_exercise
checks.description: HR should increase >15 bpm during moderate exercise

checks.name: hr_recovers
checks.description: HR should recover substantially within 15 min post-exercise

checks.name: lactate_rises
checks.description: Lactate should increase during moderate exercise

checks.name: temp_rises
checks.description: Core temperature should increase during exercise

checks.name: rr_rises
checks.description: Respiratory rate should increase during exercise

textbook_scenarios.name: sleep_wake_transition
textbook_scenarios.description: Sleep-wake transition — HR/BP dipping, temperature nadir, RR reduction

checks.name: hr_dips_during_sleep
checks.description: HR should drop during sleep compared to evening

checks.name: sbp_dips_during_sleep
checks.description: SBP should dip during sleep

checks.name: temp_drops_at_night
checks.description: Core temperature should reach nadir during night

checks.name: rr_drops_during_sleep
checks.description: Respiratory rate should decrease during sleep

textbook_scenarios.name: cortisol_awakening_response
textbook_scenarios.source: Pruessner et al. (1997); Fries et al. (2009)
textbook_scenarios.description: Cortisol awakening response — post-wake cortisol surge, ACTH-cortisol timing

checks.name: cortisol_rises_after_waking
checks.description: Cortisol should rise substantially after waking

checks.name: cortisol_morning_peak
checks.description: Cortisol should reach >15 μg/dL in the morning peak

checks.name: acth_precedes_cortisol
checks.description: ACTH peak should precede or coincide with cortisol peak

textbook_scenarios.name: dietary_carbohydrate_meal_flow
textbook_scenarios.source: Pulse flow-story contract; neural trajectory with teacher gut appearance
textbook_scenarios.description: Dietary carbohydrate from a logged mixed meal: appearance, glycemic excursion, insulin/glucagon/FFA/ghrelin/GLP-1, DIT temperature, 8h glucose recovery

checks.name: glucose_rises_after_meal
checks.description: Plasma glucose should rise meaningfully after the carb-containing meal

checks.name: appearance_leads_glucose_peak
checks.description: Peak glucose appearance (gut output) should not trail the glucose peak by much

checks.name: insulin_peak_follows_glucose
checks.description: Insulin peak should occur with or after the glycemic peak (within 30 min)

checks.name: glucagon_suppressed_postprandial
checks.description: Mean glucagon after meal should stay below fasting mean

checks.name: ffa_suppressed_antilipolysis
checks.description: Mean FFA mid-course should fall below pre-meal (insulin-mediated antilipolysis)

checks.name: ghrelin_suppressed_after_feeding
checks.description: Ghrelin should be lower in the post-absorptive window than before the meal

checks.name: glp1_incretin_pulse
checks.description: GLP-1 should show a clear post-meal pulse above fasting

checks.name: postprandial_warming
checks.description: Core temperature mean should rise slightly during the post-meal window

checks.name: glucose_returns_near_baseline
checks.description: By late simulation, glucose mean should return near the pre-meal level
